title: Unexplored Fuyutsuki and Rei
wikitext:
[]: Unexplored: Fuyutsuki & Rei             Quote Post by Eva 02 » Tue May 18, 2010 4:33 pm With much deliberation, I've decided to create this topic because of the considerable implications of the relationship between Kouzou & Ayanami. I have done a lot of research, and no one seems to have caught this: In episode 23 "Tears/Rei III" Fuyutsuki and Gendou hold a convo that I feel is significant. At about 15:04 through is the scene in question, for reference purposes. It is directly following the scene where Misato propositions herself to Shinji. Kozo Fuyutsuki > Gendo Ikari wrote: ::Rei sacrifices her life to destroy Angel, is now being processed in Dummy Plug Giant Braintank. Commander and Vicecommander are standing in front, observing:: Fuyutsuki: Rei... She is the product of my despair and at the same time, the vessel of your hope even now. In the end, I suppose it was unreasonable to expect you to forget.

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Tue May 18, 2010 5:43 pm Fuyutsuki almost certainly meant THIS: "Rei... She is the product of my despair [Yui's death (there are no necessary romantic tones here. Yui was a favorite student and she seemed to care for him very much)] and at the same time, the vessel of your [Gendo's] hope [Yui's rebirth] even now. In the end, I suppose it was unreasonable to expect you to forget [Yui, and move on with your life.]"

[]:             Quote Post by Eva 02 » Tue May 18, 2010 6:14 pm Hmmm I can see how he may have been talking about the inability to forget Yui's death. But in that rationality, wouldn't he say product of our despair? True only Gendou would be satiated in his script to be reuinited with Yui, but the statement is too vague to just dissmiss is so easily. It seems like Kozo is talking about the inabilty to foget his dispair, possibly. The product of his despair is Rei, so specifically, why? And why is it only his despair when Gendou married the woman, ultimate intimate? Gendou would be closer with Rei if it was that simple.

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Tue May 18, 2010 6:37 pm Gendo isn't despairing because in his crazy little world he hasn't really lost Yui yet. He's doing all of this because he thinks he can bring her back. It's like she's gone away on an awesome ninja vacation of slaughtering things, not like she's dead, as far as Gendo's concerned. I never got the impression that Fuyutsuki held much hope for her willing return, or even willingness to accept Gendo back after all he's done.

[]: Re: Unexplored: Fuyutsuki & Rei             Quote Post by Azathoth » Wed May 19, 2010 9:56 pm Eva 02 wrote: Fuyutsuki says Rei is the cause of despair for him, which is vague.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva 02 » Wed May 19, 2010 10:03 pm Its vague in the sense that it could go both ways. I know, despair producing Rei. But the wording just has to be able to go most ways. It is dialogue in evangelion afer all o_O

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Wed May 19, 2010 11:43 pm I'd like someone with access to the original Japanese (and someone who can interpret it) to comment there. Note that translations can create/destroy ambiguity because of the differences of languages.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Wed May 19, 2010 11:50 pm I'm moving this into the Discussion forum until there's something worth adding into the wiki. There's been a few times that Fuyu's line has come up before. To bring Yui back And an old thread: How Fuyutsuki made Rei Which was linked from here: Rei's birthplace
